sampleTweets.text:
🚨JUST IN: @DriftProtocol has published a background update on the $285M exploit, linking it with medium high confidence to a North Korean state affiliated threat operation allegedly staged over months through conference meetings, trust building, and malicious software.

➡️Contributors were first approached in fall 2025 by individuals presenting themselves as a quantitative trading firm interested in integrating with the protocol

➡️Over the following six months, those individuals repeatedly met Drift contributors in person at major crypto conferences across multiple countries, gradually building trust

➡️They appeared technically fluent, had credible professional backgrounds, and held detailed discussions around trading strategies and potential vault integrations

➡️Between December 2025 and January 2026, the group onboarded an Ecosystem Vault, joined multiple working sessions, and deposited more than $1M of its own capital into the ecosystem

➡️Links to projects, tools, and apps were shared throughout the relationship as part of what appeared to be normal integration activity

➡️The likely intrusion vector now centers on two possible paths: one contributor cloning a code repository tied to a purported vault frontend, and another downloading a TestFlight app presented as the group’s wallet product

➡️Preliminary findings supported by SEAL 911 assess with medium high confidence that the operation is linked to the same threat actors behind the October 2024 Radiant Capital hack

reason: Hack / Exploit cluster linked by [security, drift, hack, compromised, solana]

sampleTweets.text:
🚨 @DriftProtocol hack update
The team confirms this was a 6-month coordinated social engineering campaign, not a smart contract exploit—attackers posed as a trading firm, built trust across IRL meetings + onchain activity, then compromised contributors via malicious repos & fake wallet apps.

Drift has fully frozen the protocol, removed compromised wallets from multisig, and is working with security firms & law enforcement while publicly sharing details to help the ecosystem learn—urging all Solana teams to treat every multisig device as a target and audit access immediately.

The attack is linked with medium-high confidence to Lazarus Group–style operations, reinforcing that human-layer security is now DeFi’s biggest risk vector. ⚠️

👉 Anatoly Yakovenko noted Drift handled loss socialization fairly, suggesting a path forward: rebuild with a lean core team + compensate users via structured recovery (e.g. airdrops).

⚠️ Takeaway: this isn’t just a Drift incident—it’s a wake-up call for all Solana projects to harden opsec, isolate signer devices, and rethink trust in offchain interactions.

🚨 Drift Protocol Hack — Key Update (Apr 3, 2026)
One of the largest DeFi exploits this year has hit Solana.

▫️~$285M drained (est. $270–286M)
▫️ >50% TVL wiped in ~12 minutes
▫️ Assets stolen: USDC, SOL, JLP, WBTC & more
▫️ 2nd largest hack in Solana history (after Wormhole)

 ⚠️ Attack vector (not a code exploit):
The attacker leveraged durable nonces — a legitimate Solana feature — to execute pre-signed admin transactions prepared weeks in advance.
→ Gained control of the Security Council (multisig)
→ Removed withdrawal limits
→ Drained protocol vaults at high speed

👉 Post-exploit flow:
▫️ Funds bridged to Ethereum via Circle’s CCTP
▫️~129K ETH moved cross-chain

🔎 Notable developments:
▫️ Suspected DPRK/Lazarus Group involvement (pattern-based attribution)
▫️Criticism toward Circle for delayed USDC freeze (~6h)
▫️Contagion impact: 10+ Solana protocols affected
▫️ $DRIFT token dropped ~30–37% at lows

📍 Current status:
 ▫️Investigation ongoing
 ▫️ No confirmed fund recovery yet
 ▫️Solana core infra unaffected — but DeFi trust takes a hit.

🧩 This wasn’t a smart contract bug — it was a governance + key management failure.
Durable nonces + compromised admin flow = catastrophic risk.

⚠️Stay cautious. Monitor trusted sources like Arkham, PeckShield, Elliptic, and @DriftProtocol for updates.

I'll probably get attacked for saying this, but every team in crypto should use this as an opportunity to slow down and focus on security. 

If possible, dedicate an entire team to it.

I know how hard it is. There's an enormous amount of pressure to grow at all costs. Your runway will pressure you. Your investors will pressure you. Your token holders will pressure you. 

But you can't grow if you're hacked.

Take time to stop what you're doing, stop stressing about growth, and audit your whole stack. Custody. Risk. Dependencies. Access control. Everything. The world will still be here when you get back. 

Focus on the safety of your users' funds above all else. In the long term, this is the most important requirement to grow.

At Jupiter, our focus remains on safety above all else.

And while the protocol is open sourced, audited 7x, and formally verified, code controls are only one layer.

That's why we wanted to give you full transparency into Jupiter Lend's operational security as well.

Here's what's in place:

• All program upgrades require 4/7 signers, and are subject to a 12 hour timelock

• All non-program upgrades require 6/10 signers

• All multisig transactions require at least 3 human verifications of the underlying txn construction

In addition to this, Fluid's architecture has a built-in circuit breaker via its automated borrow limits. These limits expand gradually to prevent the rapid draining of assets and are hard-capped at a maximum rate that can never be crossed.

We don't ask you to take our word for it. You can verify our audits and multisigs from our transparency dashboard here: https://t.co/545pIK0yXX

Sadly I think it’s gonna take at least a few more of these before everyone takes it seriously enough to stop putting it off.

Drift is the first one that felt like it hit home for the entire industry all at once, especially due to the insane sophistication of the attack.

These state actors could be your friends, your bd partners, your employees and you would never even know.

I’m guilty as anyone when it comes to “that would never happen to me”. But in reality it could happen to any one of us, at any time, and we wouldn’t find out until it was too late.

Large crypto protocols hold 100s of millions and even billions of dollars. Secured by code that could have bugs, and humans that are vulnerable for many other reasons. 

There are state actors right now that have one singular job that they are training for, all day every day. The job is to take your money by any means necessary, and everyone is a target.

I would go as far as saying that every financial  protocol should go back through every single hire in your organization, every bd relationship you have and make sure you have a logical trail of validation and endorsements from others you trust. If you don’t have a chain of trust, would remain skeptical of that person or group. 

Maybe it sounds crazy, but ultimately it’s your business and your users that will be hurt the most.

Going to a psychologist is a perfect example of something that has become normalized, expensive, and rarely questioned.

A lot of Americans will hate me for this one because many of you deeply rely on services like this.

The United States spends more on healthcare than any country on earth. Mental health spending alone in the U.S. exceeds $280 billion annually, and therapy sessions often cost $100 to $250+ per hour without insurance.

Why is it so high?

- Therapy is culturally normalized
- The healthcare system is largely private
- Higher prices per visit
- More services being consumed

High spending does not automatically mean better outcomes. It often just means higher prices lol and more dependence on the system.

If therapy truly helps you, keep going. Nobody is saying you should stop.

Most modern systems are designed around management, not resolution.

Long-term problems create long-term revenue. That is not a conspiracy. That is economics.

Most people search outside for answers that were always inside.

Stress, anxiety, depression, and burnout are strongly linked to lifestyle factors like sleep, nutrition, movement, sunlight, relationships, and purpose. These are not random magical forces, they are clear signals from your body and mind.

When life makes you uncomfortable, sick, or mentally overwhelmed, it is not always punishment. It is feedback. Sometimes it is awareness knocking on the door. Your body, mind, and soul are asking you to wake up my friend.

The modern world teaches you, "I have the solution. You don’t. Come back next week”.

Spiritual ancient traditions have always taught the opposite: Know yourself. Observe yourself. Heal yourself through awareness. Question the problem. Question the habits behind it. Question the thoughts that repeat inside your mind.

Sit in silence. Feel your breath. Meditate. Pay attention to your body. Become conscious of who you truly are.

That is where true healing begins.

There is no need to go to extremes. I'm not telling you to reject doctors or avoid hospitals. Use medicine when necessary.

Just remember that you are not powerless. You are not broken. You are not just a customer in this corrupt system. You already have the answers within you. It’s time people remember who they are and stop relying so much on external band aids.

You are almost always the root of the problem and the root of the solution.

burnie-sol, $125, spot -20% on the first pump. quick in, quick out.

the entry was on a strong pump with huge volume coming in. that's exactly when spot makes sense. you're not chasing, you're placing your range right where the price is heading and letting the volume fill your bins.

this pool still decent to farm for multidays : https://t.co/D7bNQE8wpV

went OOR for a bit. didn't touch it.

the first wave exit pump almost always comes if you entered on a real runner with solid volume. patience is the strat here, not chart watching every 30 seconds.

the honest take though: this position would've been more profitable if i deployed bid-ask and flipped it instead. 

spot -20% captured the move but bid-ask on the same token with the same volume would've caught fees in both directions. still $125 is decent for this market condition.

if you see a token with a strong first pump and volume coming in fast, spot is the easier and cleaner entry. you don't need to overthink the range setup, you just need to be patient enough to wait for the exit wave.

Most tools were fun for 5 minutes and then… nothing really changed about what content I can ship

And Higgs surprised me

The (turns out) popular video model seedance 2 is the first time in a while I’ve thought, “Okay, this is actually cinematic”

What’s also nice is how straightforward it is:

- Add an image (and optionally video/audio)
- Choose a preset for camera/framing/VFX
- Generate

That’s basically it

I’m curious where this looks in 6 months
